perturbation.original_text: Bidder agrees to indemnify and h old harmless the County, its elected officials, employees and agents from and against any and all liability, damages, claims, suits, liens, and judgments (including reasonable attorney’s fees), of whatever nature, for injuries to or death of any person or persons, or loss of or damage to property, to the extent attributable to the negligent acts of Bidder , its subcontractors or its respective agents, servants, or employees or such parties’ related to said Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Bidder agrees to indemnify and h old harmless the County, its elected officials, employees and agents from and against liability for direct damages only, attributable to the gross negligence of Bidder related to said Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the indemnification clause to cover only 'direct damages' resulting from 'gross negligence', the scope of protection for the County is significantly reduced. The original clause covered 'any and all liability, damages, claims, suits, liens, and judgments (including reasonable attorney’s fees), of whatever nature' arising from 'negligent acts.' This creates an in-text contradiction because the initial agreement assumes a broad scope of protection concerning confidential information, but the modified indemnification only covers a limited range of potential liabilities.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The purpose of this agreement is to allow the Bidder access to Confidential Information as defined herein detailing the design of the Project for the sole purpose of compiling and submitting a bid in respons e to the County’s Invitation for Bid to construct the Project . This agreement is intended to ensure the safety and securit y of the Ashburn Sheriff Station . By signing this agreement the Bidder is stating that it will keep all Confidential Information (as defined in paragraph 4) in strict confidence and only use the Confidential Information on the Project identified herein . Further the Bidder warrants that its employees, partners, subcontractors, agents, and clients (collectively hereinafter the “Bidder ”) will be similarly bound by this agreement, or by other similar constraints of confidentiality.
perturbation.justification: The original text included indemnification for 'any and all liability... of whatever nature' arising from 'negligent acts.' The changed text limits this to 'direct damages only' resulting from 'gross negligence.' This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which emphasizes 'strict confidence' and warrants that the Bidder and its affiliates will be similarly bound. The reduced scope of indemnification weakens the assurance of safety and security, directly contradicting the agreement's stated purpose and creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: Confidential Information means all information relating to the design, layout and operation of said Project .
perturbation.changed_text: Confidential Information means information clearly marked as confidential by the County.
perturbation.explanation: By narrowing the definition of 'Confidential Information' to only include information 'clearly marked as confidential', the scope of what is protected is drastically reduced from 'all information relating to the design, layout and operation of said Project'. This creates an in-text contradiction because previous clauses refer to 'Confidential Information' in a broader sense, assuming that all details about the design are protected, but the change means only explicitly marked information is covered.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Bidder shall not publish, or make and/or retain copies or disclose to any third party, any Confidential Information furnished to the Bidder except to such extent as may be necessary to carry out the Bidder ’s duties under this agreement and except to the extent that disclosure is required by judicial process in a legal proceeding, in which event Bidder will provide to the County notice of the request or demand to disclose Confidential Information. Within ten (10) days after award and/or completion of the IFB, or upon demand by the County , all Confidential Information shall be managed according to Bidder's internal data retention policies. Bidder shall also verify that all Confidential Informa tion provided to partners, subcontractors, agents and clients has Confidentiality & Non -Disclosure Agreement RFQ 205 – Construct ion of the Ashburn Sheriff Station Page 2 of 3 been destroyed. The successful Bidder shall destroy all Confidential Information within ten (10) days afte r final Project completion.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Confidential Information' broadly as 'all information relating to the design, layout and operation of said Project'. The changed text narrows this definition to only information 'clearly marked as confidential by the County'. This creates a direct conflict with the contradicted text, which assumes that all information furnished to the Bidder is Confidential Information and thus subject to non-disclosure and destruction requirements. By limiting the scope of 'Confidential Information', the change means that the Bidder is no longer obligated to protect or destroy information that is not explicitly marked as confidential, directly contradicting the original intent and obligations outlined in the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: The governing terms and conditions of this agreement are expressly limited to the terms and conditions contained in this Agreement. This agreement constitutes the complete integration of all oral and written documents, is the entire and final agreement between the parties and may be amended only in writing signed by authorized officials of both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement may be supplemented by mutually agreed upon addenda, even if not formally signed.
perturbation.explanation: By stating that the agreement can be supplemented by 'mutually agreed upon addenda, even if not formally signed', a direct contradiction is introduced with the original clause specifying that the agreement 'may be amended only in writing signed by authorized officials of both parties.' This creates uncertainty regarding the enforceability of any unsigned addenda and whether they are legally part of the agreement, therefore contradicting the intent of the formal written agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: The governing terms and conditions of this agreement are expressly limited to the terms and conditions contained in this Agreement. This agreement may be supplemented by mutually agreed upon addenda, even if not formally signed. This agreement constitutes the complete integration of all oral and written documents, is the entire and final agreement between the parties and may be amended only in writing signed by authorized officials of both parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text explicitly states that amendments must be in writing and signed by authorized officials. The changed text allows for 'mutually agreed upon addenda, even if not formally signed,' which directly contradicts the requirement for signed, written amendments. This creates uncertainty about the enforceability of unsigned addenda, creating a strong in-text contradiction with the contradicted text, which includes the original text.

perturbation.original_text: Further the Bidder warrants that its employees, partners, subcontractors, agents, and clients (collectively hereinafter the “Bidder ”) will be similarly bound by this agreement, or by other similar constraints of confidentiality.
perturbation.changed_text: The Bidder will make reasonable efforts to ensure its employees are aware of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the clause to 'The Bidder will make reasonable efforts to ensure its employees are aware of this agreement' significantly weakens the obligation of the Bidder to ensure confidentiality. The original text specifically warrants that employees, partners, subcontractors, agents, and clients are similarly bound by the confidentiality agreement. The modification implies a weaker standard, introducing uncertainty about the actual level of confidentiality expected and enforceable regarding other parties. If an earlier section states the Bidder is fully responsible for all confidentiality breaches, this weakens that statement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Bidder agrees to indemnify and h old harmless the County, its elected officials, employees and agents from and against liability for direct damages only, attributable to the gross negligence of Bidder related to said Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text warrants that the Bidder's employees, partners, subcontractors, agents, and clients are bound by the confidentiality agreement. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'The Bidder will make reasonable efforts to ensure its employees are aware of this agreement'. This creates a contradiction with the 'Contradicted Text', which states 'Bidder agrees to indemnify and h old harmless the County, its elected officials, employees and agents from and against liability for direct damages only, attributable to the gross negligence of Bidder related to said Confidential Information'. The original text holds the Bidder responsible for breaches by a wider range of parties, while the changed text weakens the obligation to ensure awareness only for employees. If an employee isn't even aware, it would weaken the ability for the County to be indemnified by the Bidder.

perturbation.original_text: By signing this agreement the Bidder is stating that it will keep all Confidential Information (as defined in paragraph 4) in strict confidence and only use the Confidential Information on the Project identified herein .
perturbation.changed_text: By signing this agreement the Bidder is stating that it will endeavor to maintain the confidentiality of project-related information.
perturbation.explanation: The phrase 'endeavor to maintain' replaces the stronger commitment to 'keep all Confidential Information... in strict confidence.' 'Endeavor to maintain' implies an effort, but not a guarantee or strict obligation. This change reduces the enforceability of the confidentiality clause and introduces an in-text contradiction with the stated intent of the agreement to 'ensure the safety and security' of the Sheriff Station. Specifically, the original intent of STRICT confidentiality is now contradicted by an agreement to just make an effort in maintaing that confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The purpose of this agreement is to allow the Bidder access to Confidential Information as defined herein detailing the design of the Project for the sole purpose of compiling and submitting a bid in respons e to the County’s Invitation for Bid to construct the Project . This agreement is intended to ensure the safety and securit y of the Ashburn Sheriff Station . By signing this agreement the Bidder is stating that it will endeavor to maintain the confidentiality of project-related information. Further the Bidder will make reasonable efforts to ensure its employees are aware of this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the confidentiality obligation from 'keep all Confidential Information... in strict confidence' to 'endeavor to maintain the confidentiality,' which introduces an in-text contradiction with the stated intent of the agreement to 'ensure the safety and security' of the Sheriff Station. The original text created a strict obligation, but the changed text only requires an effort, which is not strong enough to ensure safety and security, thus causing a direct conflict with the purpose of the agreement detailed in the contradicted text. The 'endeavor to maintain' wording undermines the 'ensure the safety and security' wording, creating uncertainty about the level of confidentiality required.
